blocks.content: I'm not scared. I want you to know that. Not of any of it. I sat with everything you told me tonight and I kept waiting for the fear to arrive and it didn't. What arrived instead was — I don't know how to say it exactly.
Something like: of course. Of course this is what I'm choosing. Of course it's you.
Three dots. They sat for a long time.
I've been afraid of this conversation for months.
I kept imagining all the ways it could go wrong.
It went right. It went so right that I don't know what to do with that yet either.
The doctor appointments. Ethan. You said you wanted to come to the appointments.
I know you did. That's the part that broke me.
Nobody has ever — not like that. So specifically. So practically.
I'm not going anywhere, Cass. That's not a feeling. That's a decision. Already made.
